meaning: To choose between options; to decide on a course of action
example: It was difficult to make a decision without all the facts.

meaning: To do something that might be dangerous or have a bad outcome
example: She decided to take a risk and start her own business.

meaning: To do something incorrectly or in error
example: Everyone makes a mistake from time to time.

meaning: To stop working for a short time to rest
example: Let's take a break and grab some coffee.

meaning: To complete assigned school work at home
example: She always does her homework before dinner.

meaning: To talk with someone; to engage in discussion
example: We need to have a conversation about your performance.

meaning: To carefully watch, listen, or concentrate on something
example: Please pay attention when the teacher is speaking.

meaning: To do what you said you would do
example: He always keeps his promises, no matter what.

meaning: To become ill with a cold virus
example: She caught a cold after standing in the rain.

meaning: To use less time on something; to be more efficient
example: Taking the highway will save us a lot of time.

meaning: To use time doing a particular activity
example: I love spending time with my family on weekends.

meaning: To formally speak to a group of people
example: The president gave a powerful speech at the ceremony.

meaning: To help people know about an important issue
example: The campaign aims to raise awareness about climate change.

meaning: To form an opinion or judgment based on evidence
example: From the data, we can draw a clear conclusion.

meaning: To come to a mutual understanding or deal
example: After hours of debate, they finally reached an agreement.

meaning: To finish something by the required time
example: The team worked overtime to meet the project deadline.

meaning: To establish an objective or target to work toward
example: She set a goal to run a marathon by the end of the year.

meaning: To successfully reach a target you aimed for
example: With hard work, he achieved every goal he set.

meaning: To find a solution or answer to a difficulty
example: Engineers work together to solve complex problems.

meaning: To deal with something difficult
example: Every entrepreneur faces challenges when starting out.

meaning: To acquire practical knowledge through doing something
example: Internships help students gain valuable work experience.

meaning: To stop being able to wait calmly; to become frustrated
example: She finally lost patience with the slow service.

meaning: To receive a prize for excellence or achievement
example: The film won several awards at the festival.

meaning: To have a formal gathering to discuss topics
example: The company holds a weekly meeting every Monday.

meaning: To write down important information while listening
example: Students are encouraged to take notes during lectures.

meaning: To investigate and study a topic systematically
example: Scientists spend years doing research before publishing results.

meaning: To advance or improve over time
example: The patient is making great progress in recovery.

meaning: To do something in response to a situation
example: It's time to stop talking and take action.

meaning: To become lighter; to reduce body mass
example: He followed a strict diet to lose weight.

meaning: To become heavier; to increase body mass
example: Many people tend to gain weight during winter.

meaning: To stop doing something you do regularly
example: Breaking a bad habit requires real discipline.

meaning: To start doing something so regularly it becomes automatic
example: Reading every morning is a great habit to form.

meaning: To say what is real or honest; not to lie
example: It's always better to tell the truth.

meaning: To say something false; to be dishonest
example: He was caught telling a lie about his whereabouts.

meaning: To try hard to do something
example: Please make an effort to arrive on time.

meaning: To accept that you are in charge of or to blame for something
example: A good leader takes responsibility for the team's failures.

meaning: To arrange a specific time to meet someone, especially officially
example: I need to make an appointment with the doctor.

meaning: To try something even though it might not succeed
example: He decided to take a chance and ask for a promotion.

meaning: To go and see someone or a place
example: We paid a visit to our grandparents last weekend.

meaning: To earn enough money to support yourself
example: He makes a living as a freelance photographer.

meaning: To do a helpful or kind act for someone
example: Could you do me a favour and mail this letter?

meaning: To formally express dissatisfaction about something
example: She made a complaint about the poor customer service.

meaning: To look at something briefly or casually
example: Can you take a look at this document for me?

meaning: To select one option from among several
example: You need to make a choice between the two offers.

meaning: To sit down
example: Please take a seat; the doctor will be with you shortly.

meaning: To tell someone your opinion about what they should do
example: My mentor always gives me excellent advice.

meaning: To ask someone for their guidance or recommendation
example: I always seek advice before making big decisions.

meaning: To achieve the best result ever recorded in something
example: The athlete set a new world record in the 100m sprint.

meaning: To surpass the previously best recorded achievement
example: She broke the record for fastest completion of the course.

meaning: To finally reach a decision or judgment
example: After reviewing the evidence, we came to a conclusion.

meaning: To sleep for a short time during the day
example: I always take a nap after lunch to recharge.

meaning: To be moved to a higher position at work
example: She worked hard and finally got a promotion.

meaning: To manage and operate a company
example: Running a business requires dedication and strong leadership.

meaning: To begin having children; to establish a household
example: They got married and decided to start a family.

meaning: To form new friendships with people
example: It's easy to make friends when you're open and friendly.

meaning: To no longer be employed; to be let go
example: He lost his job during the economic downturn.

meaning: To obtain employment
example: It took her three months to find a job after graduation.

meaning: To attempt or sit an exam or assessment
example: Students take a test at the end of each unit.

meaning: To successfully complete an exam with a satisfactory grade
example: She studied hard and passed all her exams.

meaning: To not achieve the required score in an assessment
example: He failed the exam and had to retake it.

meaning: To book a table, room, or seat in advance
example: I made a reservation at a restaurant for our anniversary.

meaning: To formally request goods or food
example: He placed an order for new office supplies.

meaning: To hand in or send a formal written document
example: She submitted her report before the deadline.

meaning: To begin an organized effort to achieve a goal
example: The charity launched a nationwide awareness campaign.

meaning: To develop how others perceive your quality or character over time
example: She has spent years building a reputation for honesty.

meaning: To come to an agreement, especially in business
example: The two companies struck a deal after long negotiations.

meaning: To propose an idea for others to consider
example: Can I make a suggestion about how to improve the process?

meaning: To bring up a topic or issue for discussion
example: The report raises several important questions.

meaning: To make a move or action toward a goal
example: Taking the first step is always the hardest.

meaning: To be logical, clear, or understandable
example: Your explanation finally makes sense to me.

meaning: To settle a financial charge or invoice
example: He insisted on paying the bill at dinner.

meaning: To fail to finish something by the required date
example: Missing a deadline can have serious consequences at work.

meaning: To fail to take advantage of a chance
example: Don't miss this opportunity to grow your skills.

meaning: To express a clear opinion or position on an issue
example: She took a stand against workplace discrimination.

meaning: To expose yourself to danger or a bad outcome
example: If you don't prepare, you run the risk of failing.

meaning: To create a strong, lasting effect on someone
example: He made a great impression at the interview.

meaning: To cause people to notice something or someone
example: The bright colours drew attention to the display.

meaning: To use time inefficiently or on unimportant things
example: Stop wasting time on social media and focus on your work.

meaning: To formally show or explain something to a group
example: She gave a compelling presentation to the investors.

meaning: To attempt to estimate or predict without certainty
example: Take a guess at how many people attended the event.

meaning: To not tell others about private information
example: Can I trust you to keep a secret?

meaning: To express an opinion or fact clearly in discussion
example: She made a valid point during the debate.

meaning: To no longer believe a good outcome is possible
example: Even after many setbacks, he refused to lose hope.

meaning: To cause surprise or mild disapproval in others
example: His unusual decision raised a few eyebrows at the office.
